module_map.responsibility: HTTP request/response abstraction layer. Handles WSGI/ASGI environ translation, query string parsing, multipart upload handling, and serializable response objects.
URL routing and resolution. Maps URL patterns to view callbacks via regex/route matching, manages URL reversals, and resolves incoming request paths to view functions with captured parameters.

Request/response processing pipeline. Middleware classes intercept requests before view dispatch and responses before return, enabling features like CSRF protection, authentication, caching, and logging.
View functions and error handlers. Provides default error views (404, 500) and view utilities for debugging, static file serving, and i18n redirects.
Django template engine. Compiles and renders template strings/files with context variables, filters, tags, and custom loaders.
ORM query interface and transaction management. Provides high-level database abstraction via querysets and model manager, plus atomic transaction blocks.
Settings registry and configuration. Lazy-loads project settings from Django settings module and manages global configuration (databases, installed apps, middleware stack).
Signal dispatcher for decoupled event handling. Implements weak-reference observer pattern for pre/post save, delete, and custom application signals with async support.
Core utilities and exceptions. Provides signing, CSRF token generation, validation, ORM exceptions, and common utility functions used across Django.
General-purpose utilities (HTML escaping, text formatting, cache headers, lazy evaluation, decorators, date/time parsing). Lowest-level dependency layer.

pipeline_trace.description: WSGI/ASGI application receives environ dict or scope/receive/send callables. Creates HttpRequest from WSGI environ or ASGI scope.
Middleware chain processes request via process_request() hooks. CsrfViewMiddleware validates CSRF tokens, CommonMiddleware handles redirects, SecurityMiddleware adds security headers.
URLResolver.resolve(request.path_info) matches path against URLPattern regex/routes. Returns ResolverMatch with view callable, args, kwargs, and captured URL parameters.
View function/class receives HttpRequest + resolved args/kwargs. Processes business logic, queries ORM, renders templates.
QuerySet.all() / .filter() executes SQL via database connection. Wrapped in db.transaction.atomic() for atomicity. Signals (pre_save, post_save, pre_delete) emitted via dispatch.Signal.send().
Template engine renders template with context. Creates HttpResponse/JsonResponse/TemplateResponse with status, headers, content.
Middleware chain processes response via process_response() hooks. CsrfViewMiddleware adds CSRF cookie, CommonMiddleware sets cache headers.
WSGI: calls response(environ, start_response). ASGI: sends response body via send() callable. Client receives status, headers, and body.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central request/response abstraction. Every request starts as HttpRequest, passed through middleware, views, and returned as HttpResponse. Middleware, views, template system, and ORM all depend on http types.

Foundational utilities used by all modules. Provides cached_property, CaseInsensitiveMapping, HTML escaping, text formatting, cache header manipulation, timezone handling, and lazy evaluation primitives.

Process hook integration point. Middleware orchestrates request/response lifecycle, depends on http for request/response types, dispatch for signals, urls for resolver metadata, and utils for common operations.

change_proposal.feature_summary: Add an async-compatible structured logging middleware that captures per-request metadata (view name, ORM query count, response time) and emits JSON-structured log entries. Supports both sync and async views.
new_types: StructuredLoggingMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin) - main middleware class with process_request/process_response hooks
RequestMetadata(NamedTuple) - captures view_name, query_count, start_time, elapsed_ms, status_code, method, path
QueryCounterManager - patches db.connection to track query execution count per request
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow CsrfViewMiddleware pattern from django/middleware/csrf.py: (1) Inherit MiddlewareMixin; (2) implement process_request() to initialize state; (3) implement process_response() to finalize and log; (4) use request attributes (request._query_count) to store per-request state; (5) use cached_property for lazy initialization; (6) use django.utils.log.log_response() for structured logging output.
change_proposal.integration_point: Middleware stack in MIDDLEWARE setting. Positioned after SecurityMiddleware (early) and before CsrfViewMiddleware (to capture all queries). Hooks into db.connection.queries to count ORM queries. Emits via logging.getLogger('django.request') with JSON formatter.
risks: Query counting adds per-request overhead if not cached; iterating request.META on every request is O(n) in header count. Mitigate by storing query count in request._state to avoid repeated introspection.

Async support requires wrapping async views with sync_to_async; if view is already async, middleware process_response() is sync, creating context-switch cost. Requires careful testing with AsyncView classes.

Signal emission during query execution (pre_save/post_save) can hide true query count if signals spawn additional queries. Mitigate by resetting counter before signal emission or using database.Cursor-level instrumentation.
Memory leak risk if request._state is not properly cleaned up after response closes. Must implement process_exception() and ensure QueryCounterManager.__exit__() cleans state.
JSON serialization of metadata dict could fail if view name is non-ASCII or contains special characters. Sanitize view_name via str.encode('utf-8', errors='replace').

tool_usage.params: /tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django with summary=true, max_depth=2 to map top-level package structure and file organization
/tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/http/request.py - extracted HttpRequest class definition, QueryDict, MediaType; identified 74 functions and 6 classes for request parsing and header handling
/tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/urls/resolvers.py - extracted URLResolver, URLPattern, ResolverMatch classes; identified 53 functions for URL pattern matching and resolution logic
/tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/middleware/csrf.py - extracted CsrfViewMiddleware pattern: process_request, process_view, process_response hooks; 25 functions for token validation
/tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/db/transaction.py - extracted Atomic context manager, transaction control functions; identified 19 functions for savepoint and rollback management
/tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/dispatch/dispatcher.py - extracted Signal class with send/asend/send_robust methods; identified 26 functions and async support for observer pattern
/tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/http/response.py - extracted HttpResponse, StreamingHttpResponse, JsonResponse hierarchy; identified 66 functions for response rendering and header serialization
/tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/apps/config.py - extracted AppConfig class; identified app initialization pattern and model discovery via import_models()
HttpRequest (follow_depth=1) - validated that HttpRequest is instantiated in WSGI application entry point and passed through middleware chain
URLResolver (follow_depth=1) - verified URLResolver self-reference pattern for nested URL configurations and identified resolve() method as entry point from middleware
